{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ootori jinja hanahata adachi 2014.jpg|260px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|48|33.5|N|139|49|13|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Adachi|ja|足立区}}, {{ill|Hanahata (Adachi)|ja|花畑 (足立区)}} 7-15-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude_min = 48 | Latitude_sec = 33.5 | Longitude = 139 | Longitude_min = 49 | Longitude_sec = 13 | Label_position = bottom | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} | Establishment = {{ill|Hōji|ja|宝治}} period (1247–1249) | Main_building = | Alternate_names = Hanahata Ōtori Shrine | Festival = | Ceremonies = {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞}}
{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Adachi|ja|足立区}}, Tokyo. It serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}} (guardian deity) of the Hanahata area and is also known as '''Hanahata Ōtori Shrine''' (花畑大鷲神社). It is considered the origin of the well-known {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} markets, with its head shrine being {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}. The shrine is featured in the {{ill|Edo meisho zue|ja|江戸名所図会}}.{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=521}} == Overview == Surrounded by old and large trees, the shrine is situated in a serene environment teeming with various seasonal flora and fauna. Though typically tranquil, it attracts many visitors during {{ill|Hatsumōde|ja|初詣}} (New Year’s visits) and {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}} celebrations in November. During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ({{ill|Sangaku|ja|算額}}) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the {{ill|Hōji|ja|宝治}} period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the {{ill|Later Three-Year War|ja|後三年の役}}, the Heian-period military commander {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光}} prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Kōda|ja|棟札}} dated to the first year of {{ill|Kan’ei|ja|寛永}} (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} (1659). * During the {{ill|Shinbutsu shūgō|ja|神仏習合|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the {{ill|Kubota Domain|ja|久保田藩}}’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎}}. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光}} was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from {{ill|Yatsuka Station|ja|谷塚駅}} on the {{ill|Tōbu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title={{ill|Edo Meisho Zue|ja|江戸名所図会}}|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社}} * {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Adachi Ward)}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from {{ill|Yatsuka Station|ja|谷塚駅}} on the {{ill|Tōbu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ootori jinja hanahata adachi 2014.jpg|260px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|48|33.5|N|139|49|13|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Adachi|ja|足立区}}, {{ill|Hanahata (Adachi)|ja|花畑 (足立区)}} 7-15-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude_min = 48 | Latitude_sec = 33.5 | Longitude = 139 | Longitude_min = 49 | Longitude_sec = 13 | Label_position = bottom | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} | Establishment = {{ill|Hōji|ja|宝治}} period (1247–1249) | Main_building = | Alternate_names = Hanahata Ōtori Shrine | Festival = | Ceremonies = {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞}}
{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Adachi|ja|足立区}}, Tokyo. It serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}} (guardian deity) of the Hanahata area and is also known as '''Hanahata Ōtori Shrine''' (花畑大鷲神社). It is considered the origin of the well-known {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} markets, with its head shrine being {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}. The shrine is featured in the {{ill|Edo meisho zue|ja|江戸名所図会}}.{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=521}} == Overview == Surrounded by old and large trees, the shrine is situated in a serene environment teeming with various seasonal flora and fauna. Though typically tranquil, it attracts many visitors during {{ill|Hatsumōde|ja|初詣}} (New Year’s visits) and {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}} celebrations in November. During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ({{ill|Sangaku|ja|算額}}) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the {{ill|Hōji|ja|宝治}} period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the {{ill|Later Three-Year War|ja|後三年の役}}, the Heian-period military commander {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光}} prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Kōda|ja|棟札}} dated to the first year of {{ill|Kan’ei|ja|寛永}} (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} (1659). * During the {{ill|Shinbutsu shūgō|ja|神仏習合|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the {{ill|Kubota Domain|ja|久保田藩}}’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎}}. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光}} was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from {{ill|Yatsuka Station|ja|谷塚駅}} on the {{ill|Tōbu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title={{ill|Edo Meisho Zue|ja|江戸名所図会}}|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社}} * {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{citation needed|Otori Shrine (Adachi Ward)}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ootori jinja hanahata adachi 2014.jpg|260px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|48|33.5|N|139|49|13|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Adachi|ja|足立区}}, {{ill|Hanahata (Adachi)|ja|花畑 (足立区)}} 7-15-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude_min = 48 | Latitude_sec = 33.5 | Longitude = 139 | Longitude_min = 49 | Longitude_sec = 13 | Label_position = bottom | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} | Establishment = {{ill|Hōji|ja|宝治}} period (1247–1249) | Main_building = | Alternate_names = Hanahata Ōtori Shrine | Festival = | Ceremonies = {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞}}
{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Adachi|ja|足立区}}, Tokyo. It serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}} (guardian deity) of the Hanahata area and is also known as '''Hanahata Ōtori Shrine''' (花畑大鷲神社). It is considered the origin of the well-known {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} markets, with its head shrine being {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}}. The shrine is featured in the {{ill|Edo meisho zue|ja|江戸名所図会}}.{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=521}} == Overview == Surrounded by old and large trees, the shrine is situated in a serene environment teeming with various seasonal flora and fauna. Though typically tranquil, it attracts many visitors during {{ill|Hatsumōde|ja|初詣}} (New Year’s visits) and {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三}} celebrations in November. During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ({{ill|Sangaku|ja|算額}}) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the {{ill|Hōji|ja|宝治}} period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the {{ill|Later Three-Year War|ja|後三年の役}}, the Heian-period military commander {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光}} prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Kōda|ja|棟札}} dated to the first year of {{ill|Kan’ei|ja|寛永}} (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} (1659). * During the {{ill|Shinbutsu shūgō|ja|神仏習合|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the {{ill|Kubota Domain|ja|久保田藩}}’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|ja|左甚五郎}}. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|ja|源義光}} was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ({{ill|Marine Day|ja|海の日}}): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from {{ill|Yatsuka Station|ja|谷塚駅}} on the {{ill|Tōbu Isesaki Line|ja|東武伊勢崎線}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title={{ill|Edo Meisho Zue|ja|江戸名所図会}}|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社}} * {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title={{ill|Edo Meisho Zue|ja|江戸名所図会}}|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社}} * {{ill|Ōtori Taisha|ja|大鳥大社|1=Ōtori taisha}} revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 大鷲神社 |画像 = [[File:Ootori jinja hanahata adachi 2014.jpg|260px]]
鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|48|33.5|N|139|49|13|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[足立区]][[花畑 (足立区)|花畑]]七丁目15番1号 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分=48|緯度秒=33.5 |経度度 = 139|経度分=49|経度秒=13 |ラベル位置 = bottom |地図 = Tokyo city |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |社格 = |創建 = [[宝治]]年間 |本殿 = |別名 = 花畑大鷲神社 |例祭 = |神事 = [[三匹獅子舞]]
[[酉の市]] }} '''大鷲神社'''（おおとりじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[足立区]]で地元・花畑地区の[[産土神]]とされる[[神社]]で、'''花畑大鷲神社'''（はなはたおおとりじんじゃ）とも称される。門前市は、各地で行われる「[[酉の市]]」の発祥といわれており、総本社は「[[大鳥大社]]」とされる。『[[江戸名所図会]]』に掲載されている{{sfn|江戸名所図会|1927|p=521}}。 ==概要== 古木や巨木に囲まれた静かな環境のなかにあり、四季おりおりにさまざまな植物や昆虫、野鳥などが見られる。ふだんは静寂であるが、[[正月]]の[[初詣]]、[[11月]]の[[七五三]]などの時期は参拝客でにぎわう。また、酉の市の夜には[[花火]]が打ち上げられる。 江戸時代後期には周辺各地の[[和算家]]より[[算額]]を奉納された記録があり、一部は現存している。天保4年（[[1833年]]）に金杉清常の門人たちが奉納した算額は「花畑大鷲神社算額」として足立区登録有形文化財になっている（1985年11月12日登録）{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=花畑大鷲神社算額|publisher=足立区 |accessdate=2022-08-22}}。 ==歴史== ===由来=== この神社の創建は[[宝治]]年間（[[1247年]]-[[1249年]]）とされる足立史談会（1978年）231頁。。[[平安時代]]後期の武将[[源義光]]が、[[後三年の役]]のおり、兄の[[源義家]]を助けるため[[東北地方]]へ向かう途中、この地で戦勝を祈願した際に見かけた[[鷲]]を祀ったのがこの神社の縁起であるという足立史談会編（1992年）162頁。。古くは鷲明神と称されていたが、明治以降鷲神社と改められ、のち大鷲神社を正式名称とした。ちなみに明治初年に初めて地番が施行された際、当神社は「花又村一番地」となった。 *[[寛永]]元年（1624年）の[[棟札]]写に「再建」とあるところから、それ以前から神社が存在していたものと考えられる。 *現存する石造物では[[万治]]2年（1659年）銘のものが最古である。 *[[神仏習合]]の時代には[[正覚院 (足立区)|正覚院]]が別当寺であった。 *現在の社は安政年間、秋田[[久保田藩]]主[[佐竹氏]]の寄進とされ、[[左甚五郎]]の弟子が彫ったとされる柱の彫刻がある。 ===祭神=== 現在の祭神[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]は[[明治]]以降に祭神とされた神で、当神社に関する[[江戸時代]]の地誌には一切現れない。実質的な祭神は[[源義光]]と考えられ、佐竹氏はじめ義光の後裔を称する大名や武家に崇敬された。 ==行事== *7月第3日曜日（[[海の日]]） - 花畑大鷲神社獅子舞（足立区指定無形民俗文化財）[https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/documents/55937/20220225_0203.pdf あだち広報 2022年（令和4年）2月25日（第1874号）] 足立区、2022年4月20日閲覧。。[[三匹獅子舞]]が演じられる（大鷲神社祈祷獅子足立史談会（1978年）232頁。）。 *酉の市 == 交通アクセス == *[[東武伊勢崎線]][[谷塚駅]]より徒歩27分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=経路案内}}）。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 足立史談会　『足立区の歴史』、名著出版、1978年、230-232頁。 * 足立史談会編　『足立区史跡散歩』、[[学生社]]、1992年、162-166頁。 * {{cite book|和書|title=[[江戸名所図会]]|volume=3|author=斎藤幸雄|publisher=有朋堂書店|year=1927|chapter=卷之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521-524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|江戸名所図会|1927}}}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[鷲神社]] * [[大鳥大社]] == 外部リンク == *[http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ 大鷲神社] - 公式サイト {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとりしんしや あたちく}} [[Category:足立区の神社]] [[Category:源義光]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル]]

{{神社 |名称 = 大鷲神社 |画像 = [[File:Ootori jinja hanahata adachi 2014.jpg|260px]]
鳥居
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|48|33.5|N|139|49|13|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[足立区]][[花畑 (足立区)|花畑]]七丁目15番1号 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分=48|緯度秒=33.5 |経度度 = 139|経度分=49|経度秒=13 |ラベル位置 = bottom |地図 = Tokyo city |祭神 = [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]] |社格 = |創建 = [[宝治]]年間 |本殿 = |別名 = 花畑大鷲神社 |例祭 = |神事 = [[三匹獅子舞]]
[[酉の市]] }} '''大鷲神社'''（おおとりじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[足立区]]で地元・花畑地区の[[産土神]]とされる[[神社]]で、'''花畑大鷲神社'''（はなはたおおとりじんじゃ）とも称される。門前市は、各地で行われる「[[酉の市]]」の発祥といわれており、総本社は「[[大鳥大社]]」とされる。『[[江戸名所図会]]』に掲載されている{{sfn|江戸名所図会|1927|p=521}}。 ==概要== 古木や巨木に囲まれた静かな環境のなかにあり、四季おりおりにさまざまな植物や昆虫、野鳥などが見られる。ふだんは静寂であるが、[[正月]]の[[初詣]]、[[11月]]の[[七五三]]などの時期は参拝客でにぎわう。また、酉の市の夜には[[花火]]が打ち上げられる。 江戸時代後期には周辺各地の[[和算家]]より[[算額]]を奉納された記録があり、一部は現存している。天保4年（[[1833年]]）に金杉清常の門人たちが奉納した算額は「花畑大鷲神社算額」として足立区登録有形文化財になっている（1985年11月12日登録）{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=花畑大鷲神社算額|publisher=足立区 |accessdate=2022-08-22}}。 ==歴史== ===由来=== この神社の創建は[[宝治]]年間（[[1247年]]-[[1249年]]）とされる足立史談会（1978年）231頁。。[[平安時代]]後期の武将[[源義光]]が、[[後三年の役]]のおり、兄の[[源義家]]を助けるため[[東北地方]]へ向かう途中、この地で戦勝を祈願した際に見かけた[[鷲]]を祀ったのがこの神社の縁起であるという足立史談会編（1992年）162頁。。古くは鷲明神と称されていたが、明治以降鷲神社と改められ、のち大鷲神社を正式名称とした。ちなみに明治初年に初めて地番が施行された際、当神社は「花又村一番地」となった。 *[[寛永]]元年（1624年）の[[棟札]]写に「再建」とあるところから、それ以前から神社が存在していたものと考えられる。 *現存する石造物では[[万治]]2年（1659年）銘のものが最古である。 *[[神仏習合]]の時代には[[正覚院 (足立区)|正覚院]]が別当寺であった。 *現在の社は安政年間、秋田[[久保田藩]]主[[佐竹氏]]の寄進とされ、[[左甚五郎]]の弟子が彫ったとされる柱の彫刻がある。 ===祭神=== 現在の祭神[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]は[[明治]]以降に祭神とされた神で、当神社に関する[[江戸時代]]の地誌には一切現れない。実質的な祭神は[[源義光]]と考えられ、佐竹氏はじめ義光の後裔を称する大名や武家に崇敬された。 ==行事== *7月第3日曜日（[[海の日]]） - 花畑大鷲神社獅子舞（足立区指定無形民俗文化財）[https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/documents/55937/20220225_0203.pdf あだち広報 2022年（令和4年）2月25日（第1874号）] 足立区、2022年4月20日閲覧。。[[三匹獅子舞]]が演じられる（大鷲神社祈祷獅子足立史談会（1978年）232頁。）。 *酉の市 == 交通アクセス == *[[東武伊勢崎線]][[谷塚駅]]より徒歩27分（{{OSM経路図|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=経路案内}}）。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 足立史談会　『足立区の歴史』、名著出版、1978年、230-232頁。 * 足立史談会編　『足立区史跡散歩』、[[学生社]]、1992年、162-166頁。 * {{cite book|和書|title=[[江戸名所図会]]|volume=3|author=斎藤幸雄|publisher=有朋堂書店|year=1927|chapter=卷之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521-524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|江戸名所図会|1927}}}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[鷲神社]] * [[大鳥大社]] == 外部リンク == *[http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ 大鷲神社] - revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ootori jinja hanahata adachi 2014.jpg|260px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|48|33.5|N|139|49|13|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], {{ill|Hanahata (Adachi)|ja|花畑 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata (Adachi)|qq=}} 7-15-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude_min = 48 | Latitude_sec = 33.5 | Longitude = 139 | Longitude_min = 49 | Longitude_sec = 13 | Label_position = bottom | Map = Tokyo city | deity = [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | Establishment = [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249) | Main_building = | Alternate_names = Hanahata Ōtori Shrine | Festival = | Ceremonies = {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}}
{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], Tokyo. It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Hanahata area and is also known as '''Hanahata Ōtori Shrine''' (花畑大鷲神社). It is considered the origin of the well-known {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} markets, with its head shrine being [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]]. The shrine is featured in the [[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo meisho zue]].{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=521}} == Overview == Surrounded by old and large trees, the shrine is situated in a serene environment teeming with various seasonal flora and fauna. Though typically tranquil, it attracts many visitors during [[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]] (New Year’s visits) and [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] celebrations in November. During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ([[:en:Sangaku|Sangaku]]) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the [[:en:Gosannen War|Later Three-Year War]], the Heian-period military commander [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|qq=}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan’ei]] (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] (1659). * During the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|qq=}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the [[:en:Kubota Domain|Kubota Domain]]’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]]. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|qq=}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Adachi]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)]] [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ootori jinja hanahata adachi 2014.jpg|260px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|48|33.5|N|139|49|13|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], {{ill|Hanahata (Adachi)|ja|花畑 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata (Adachi)|qq=}} 7-15-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude_min = 48 | Latitude_sec = 33.5 | Longitude = 139 | Longitude_min = 49 | Longitude_sec = 13 | Label_position = bottom | Map = Tokyo city | deity = [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | Establishment = [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249) | Main_building = | Alternate_names = Hanahata Ōtori Shrine | Festival = | Ceremonies = {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}}
{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], Tokyo. It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Hanahata area and is also known as '''Hanahata Ōtori Shrine''' (花畑大鷲神社). It is considered the origin of the well-known {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} markets, with its head shrine being [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]]. The shrine is featured in the [[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo meisho zue]].{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=521}} == Overview == Surrounded by old and large trees, the shrine is situated in a serene environment teeming with various seasonal flora and fauna. Though typically tranquil, it attracts many visitors during [[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]] (New Year’s visits) and [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] celebrations in November. During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ([[:en:Sangaku|Sangaku]]) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the [[:en:Gosannen War|Later Three-Year War]], the Heian-period military commander [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|qq=}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan’ei]] (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] (1659). * During the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|qq=}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the [[:en:Kubota Domain|Kubota Domain]]’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]]. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|qq=}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Adachi]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)]] [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル]]

Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Adachi]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)]] [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル]] [[Category:源義光]] [[Category:足立区の神社]]

revision.comment: Bot: update category → Shrines in Adachi, Tokyo
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ootori jinja hanahata adachi 2014.jpg|260px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|48|33.5|N|139|49|13|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], {{ill|Hanahata (Adachi)|ja|花畑 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata (Adachi)|qq=}} 7-15-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude_min = 48 | Latitude_sec = 33.5 | Longitude = 139 | Longitude_min = 49 | Longitude_sec = 13 | Label_position = bottom | Map = Tokyo city | deity = [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | Establishment = [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249) | Main_building = | Alternate_names = Hanahata Ōtori Shrine | Festival = | Ceremonies = {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}}
{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], Tokyo. It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Hanahata area and is also known as '''Hanahata Ōtori Shrine''' (花畑大鷲神社). It is considered the origin of the well-known {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} markets, with its head shrine being [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]]. The shrine is featured in the [[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo meisho zue]].{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=521}} == Overview == Surrounded by old and large trees, the shrine is situated in a serene environment teeming with various seasonal flora and fauna. Though typically tranquil, it attracts many visitors during [[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]] (New Year’s visits) and [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] celebrations in November. During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ([[:en:Sangaku|Sangaku]]) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the [[:en:Gosannen War|Later Three-Year War]], the Heian-period military commander [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|qq=}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan’ei]] (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] (1659). * During the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|qq=}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the [[:en:Kubota Domain|Kubota Domain]]’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]]. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|qq=}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Adachi]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)]] [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル]] [[Category:源義光]] [[Category:Shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]]

Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Adachi]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)]] [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru�]] [[Category:源義光]] [[Category:Shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ootori jinja hanahata adachi 2014.jpg|260px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|48|33.5|N|139|49|13|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], {{ill|Hanahata (Adachi)|ja|花畑 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata (Adachi)|qq=}} 7-15-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude_min = 48 | Latitude_sec = 33.5 | Longitude = 139 | Longitude_min = 49 | Longitude_sec = 13 | Label_position = bottom | Map = Tokyo city | deity = [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | Establishment = [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249) | Main_building = | Alternate_names = Hanahata Ōtori Shrine | Festival = | Ceremonies = {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}}
{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], Tokyo. It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Hanahata area and is also known as '''Hanahata Ōtori Shrine''' (花畑大鷲神社). It is considered the origin of the well-known {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} markets, with its head shrine being [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]]. The shrine is featured in the [[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo meisho zue]].{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=521}} == Overview == Surrounded by old and large trees, the shrine is situated in a serene environment teeming with various seasonal flora and fauna. Though typically tranquil, it attracts many visitors during [[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]] (New Year’s visits) and [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] celebrations in November. During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ([[:en:Sangaku|Sangaku]]) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the [[:en:Gosannen War|Later Three-Year War]], the Heian-period military commander [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|qq=}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan’ei]] (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] (1659). * During the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|qq=}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the [[:en:Kubota Domain|Kubota Domain]]’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]]. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|qq=}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Adachi]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)]] [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:源義光]] [[Category:Shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}}

Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Adachi]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)]] [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ootori jinja hanahata adachi 2014.jpg|260px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|48|33.5|N|139|49|13|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], {{ill|Hanahata (Adachi)|ja|花畑 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata (Adachi)|qq=}} 7-15-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude_min = 48 | Latitude_sec = 33.5 | Longitude = 139 | Longitude_min = 49 | Longitude_sec = 13 | Label_position = bottom | Map = Tokyo city | deity = [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | Establishment = [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249) | Main_building = | Alternate_names = Hanahata Ōtori Shrine | Festival = | Ceremonies = {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}}
{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], Tokyo. It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Hanahata area and is also known as '''Hanahata Ōtori Shrine''' (花畑大鷲神社). It is considered the origin of the well-known {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} markets, with its head shrine being [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]]. The shrine is featured in the [[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo meisho zue]].{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=521}} == Overview == Surrounded by old and large trees, the shrine is situated in a serene environment teeming with various seasonal flora and fauna. Though typically tranquil, it attracts many visitors during [[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]] (New Year’s visits) and [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] celebrations in November. During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ([[:en:Sangaku|Sangaku]]) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the [[:en:Gosannen War|Later Three-Year War]], the Heian-period military commander [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan’ei]] (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] (1659). * During the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|qq=}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the [[:en:Kubota Domain|Kubota Domain]]’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]]. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Adachi]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)]] [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}}

Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)]] [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11442008]] Wikidata name: Ōtori-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ootori jinja hanahata adachi 2014.jpg|260px]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|48|33.5|N|139|49|13|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], {{ill|Hanahata (Adachi)|ja|花畑 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata (Adachi)|qq=}} 7-15-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude_min = 48 | Latitude_sec = 33.5 | Longitude = 139 | Longitude_min = 49 | Longitude_sec = 13 | Label_position = bottom | Map = Tokyo city | deity = [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | Establishment = [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249) | Main_building = | Alternate_names = Hanahata Ōtori Shrine | Festival = | Ceremonies = {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}}
{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], Tokyo. It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Hanahata area and is also known as '''Hanahata Ōtori Shrine''' (花畑大鷲神社). It is considered the origin of the well-known {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} markets, with its head shrine being [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]]. The shrine is featured in the [[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo meisho zue]].{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=521}} == Overview == Surrounded by old and large trees, the shrine is situated in a serene environment teeming with various seasonal flora and fauna. Though typically tranquil, it attracts many visitors during [[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]] (New Year’s visits) and [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] celebrations in November. During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ([[:en:Sangaku|Sangaku]]) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the [[:en:Gosannen War|Later Three-Year War]], the Heian-period military commander [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan’ei]] (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] (1659). * During the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|qq=}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the [[:en:Kubota Domain|Kubota Domain]]’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]]. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[sn:Ōtori Shrine (Adachi Ward)]] [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}}

The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. [[sn:commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)]][[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11442008]] Wikidata name: Ōtori-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ootori jinja hanahata adachi 2014.jpg | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], {{ill|Hanahata (Adachi)|ja|花畑 (足立区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata (Adachi)|qq=|1=Hanahata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615595}} 7-15-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude_min = 48 | Latitude_sec = 33.5 | Longitude = 139 | Longitude_min = 49 | Longitude_sec = 13 | Label_position = bottom | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} | Establishment = [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249) | Main_building = | Alternate_names = Hanahata Ōtori Shrine | Festival = | Ceremonies = {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}}
{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Hanahata|WD=Q11615595}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.79930278|N|139.81150556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], Tokyo. It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Hanahata area and is also known as '''Hanahata Ōtori Shrine''' (花畑大鷲神社). It is considered the origin of the well-known {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} markets, with its head shrine being [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]]. The shrine is featured in the [[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo meisho zue]].{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=521}} == Overview == Surrounded by old and large trees, the shrine is situated in a serene environment teeming with various seasonal flora and fauna. Though typically tranquil, it attracts many visitors during [[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]] (New Year’s visits) and [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] celebrations in November. During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ([[:en:Sangaku|Sangaku]]) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the [[:en:Gosannen War|Later Three-Year War]], the Heian-period military commander [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan’ei]] (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] (1659). * During the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|qq=|1=Shogakuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545644}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the [[:en:Kubota Domain|Kubota Domain]]’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]]. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11442008}}
