Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]]. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11442008}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)]][[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11442008]] Wikidata name: Ōtori-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ootori jinja hanahata adachi 2014.jpg | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], {{ill|Hanahata (Adachi)|ja|花畑 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata (Adachi)|1=Hanahata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615595}} 7-15-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude_min = 48 | Latitude_sec = 33.5 | Longitude = 139 | Longitude_min = 49 | Longitude_sec = 13 | Label_position = bottom | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} | Establishment = [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249) | Main_building = | Alternate_names = Hanahata Ōtori Shrine | Festival = | Ceremonies = {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}}
{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Hanahata|WD=Q11615595}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.79930278|N|139.81150556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], Tokyo. It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Hanahata area and is also known as '''Hanahata Ōtori Shrine''' (花畑大鷲神社). It is considered the origin of the well-known {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} markets, with its head shrine being [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]]. The shrine is featured in the [[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo meisho zue]].{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=521}} == Overview == Surrounded by old and large trees, the shrine is situated in a serene environment teeming with various seasonal flora and fauna. Though typically tranquil, it attracts many visitors during [[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]] (New Year’s visits) and [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] celebrations in November. During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ([[:en:Sangaku|Sangaku]]) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the [[:en:Gosannen War|Later Three-Year War]], the Heian-period military commander [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan’ei]] (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] (1659). * During the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|1=Shogakuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545644}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the [[:en:Kubota Domain|Kubota Domain]]’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]]. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11442008}}

This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan’ei]] (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] (1659). * During the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|1=Shogakuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545644}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the [[:en:Kubota Domain|Kubota Domain]]’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]]. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11442008}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Hanahata|WD=Q11615595}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.79930278|N|139.81150556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], Tokyo. It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Hanahata area and is also known as '''Hanahata Ōtori Shrine''' (花畑大鷲神社). It is considered the origin of the well-known {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} markets, with its head shrine being [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]]. The shrine is featured in the [[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo meisho zue]].{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=521}} == Overview == Surrounded by old and large trees, the shrine is situated in a serene environment teeming with various seasonal flora and fauna. Though typically tranquil, it attracts many visitors during [[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]] (New Year’s visits) and [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] celebrations in November. During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ([[:en:Sangaku|Sangaku]]) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan’ei]] (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] (1659). * During the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|1=Shogakuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545644}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the [[:en:Kubota Domain|Kubota Domain]]’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]]. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan’ei]] (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] (1659). * During the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|1=Shogakuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545644}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the [[:en:Kubota Domain|Kubota Domain]]’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]]. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11442008}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)]][[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11442008]] Wikidata name: Ōtori-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ootori jinja hanahata adachi 2014.jpg | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], {{ill|Hanahata (Adachi)|ja|花畑 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata (Adachi)|1=Hanahata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615595}} 7-15-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude_min = 48 | Latitude_sec = 33.5 | Longitude = 139 | Longitude_min = 49 | Longitude_sec = 13 | Label_position = bottom | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} | Establishment = [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249) | Main_building = | Alternate_names = Hanahata Ōtori Shrine | Festival = | Ceremonies = {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}}
{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Hanahata|WD=Q11615595}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.79930278|N|139.81150556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], Tokyo. It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Hanahata area and is also known as '''Hanahata Ōtori Shrine''' (花畑大鷲神社). It is considered the origin of the well-known {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} markets, with its head shrine being [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]]. The shrine is featured in the [[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo meisho zue]].{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=521}} == Overview == Surrounded by old and large trees, the shrine is situated in a serene environment teeming with various seasonal flora and fauna. Though typically tranquil, it attracts many visitors during [[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]] (New Year’s visits) and [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] celebrations in November. During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ([[:en:Sangaku|Sangaku]]) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the [[:en:Gosannen War|Later Three-Year War]], the Heian-period military commander [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan’ei]] (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] (1659). * During the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|1=Shogakuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545644}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the [[:en:Kubota Domain|Kubota Domain]]’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]]. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11442008}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ([[:en:Sangaku|Sangaku]]) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the [[:en:Gosannen War|Later Three-Year War]], the Heian-period military commander [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan’ei]] (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] (1659). * During the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|1=Shogakuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545644}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the [[:en:Kubota Domain|Kubota Domain]]’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]]. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11442008}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11442008}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)]][[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11442008]] Wikidata name: Ōtori-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ootori jinja hanahata adachi 2014.jpg | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], {{ill|Hanahata (Adachi)|ja|花畑 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata (Adachi)|1=Hanahata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615595}} 7-15-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude_min = 48 | Latitude_sec = 33.5 | Longitude = 139 | Longitude_min = 49 | Longitude_sec = 13 | Label_position = bottom | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} | Establishment = [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249) | Main_building = | Alternate_names = Hanahata Ōtori Shrine | Festival = | Ceremonies = {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}}
{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Hanahata|WD=Q11615595}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.79930278|N|139.81150556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], Tokyo. It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Hanahata area and is also known as '''Hanahata Ōtori Shrine''' (花畑大鷲神社). It is considered the origin of the well-known {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} markets, with its head shrine being [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]]. The shrine is featured in the [[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo meisho zue]].{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=521}} == Overview == Surrounded by old and large trees, the shrine is situated in a serene environment teeming with various seasonal flora and fauna. Though typically tranquil, it attracts many visitors during [[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]] (New Year’s visits) and [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] celebrations in November. During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ([[:en:Sangaku|Sangaku]]) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the [[:en:Gosannen War|Later Three-Year War]], the Heian-period military commander [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan’ei]] (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] (1659). * During the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|1=Shogakuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545644}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the [[:en:Kubota Domain|Kubota Domain]]’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]]. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11442008}}

During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ([[:en:Sangaku|Sangaku]]) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the [[:en:Gosannen War|Later Three-Year War]], the Heian-period military commander [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan’ei]] (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] (1659). * During the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|1=Shogakuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545644}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the [[:en:Kubota Domain|Kubota Domain]]’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]]. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11442008}}

[[commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)]][[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)]][[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)]][[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11442008]] Wikidata name: Ōtori-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ootori jinja hanahata adachi 2014.jpg | location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], {{ill|Hanahata (Adachi)|ja|花畑 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata (Adachi)|1=Hanahata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615595}} 7-15-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude_min = 48 | Latitude_sec = 33.5 | Longitude = 139 | Longitude_min = 49 | Longitude_sec = 13 | Label_position = bottom | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} | Establishment = [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249) | Main_building = | Alternate_names = Hanahata Ōtori Shrine | Festival = | Ceremonies = {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}}
{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Hanahata|WD=Q11615595}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.79930278|N|139.81150556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], Tokyo. It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Hanahata area and is also known as '''Hanahata Ōtori Shrine''' (花畑大鷲神社). It is considered the origin of the well-known {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} markets, with its head shrine being [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]]. The shrine is featured in the [[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo meisho zue]].{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=521}} == Overview == Surrounded by old and large trees, the shrine is situated in a serene environment teeming with various seasonal flora and fauna. Though typically tranquil, it attracts many visitors during [[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]] (New Year’s visits) and [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] celebrations in November. During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ([[:en:Sangaku|Sangaku]]) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the [[:en:Gosannen War|Later Three-Year War]], the Heian-period military commander [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan’ei]] (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] (1659). * During the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|1=Shogakuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545644}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the [[:en:Kubota Domain|Kubota Domain]]’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]]. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] }} [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11442008}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

The shrine is featured in the [[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo meisho zue]].{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=521}} == Overview == Surrounded by old and large trees, the shrine is situated in a serene environment teeming with various seasonal flora and fauna. Though typically tranquil, it attracts many visitors during [[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]] (New Year’s visits) and [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] celebrations in November. During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ([[:en:Sangaku|Sangaku]]) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the [[:en:Gosannen War|Later Three-Year War]], the Heian-period military commander [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan’ei]] (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] (1659). * During the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|1=Shogakuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545644}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the [[:en:Kubota Domain|Kubota Domain]]’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]]. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11442008}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]]

It serves as the [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] (guardian deity) of the Hanahata area and is also known as '''Hanahata Ōtori Shrine''' (花畑大鷲神社). It is considered the origin of the well-known {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} markets, with its head shrine being [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]]. The shrine is featured in the [[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo meisho zue]].{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=521}} == Overview == Surrounded by old and large trees, the shrine is situated in a serene environment teeming with various seasonal flora and fauna. Though typically tranquil, it attracts many visitors during [[:en:Hatsumōde|Hatsumōde]] (New Year’s visits) and [[:en:Shichi-Go-San|Shichi-Go-San]] celebrations in November. During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ([[:en:Sangaku|Sangaku]]) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the [[:en:Hōji (era)|Hōji]] period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the [[:en:Gosannen War|Later Three-Year War]], the Heian-period military commander [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan’ei]] (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] (1659). * During the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|1=Shogakuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545644}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the [[:en:Kubota Domain|Kubota Domain]]’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of [[:en:Hidari Jingorō|Hidari Jingorō]]. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ([[:en:Marine Day|Marine Day]]): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from [[:en:Yatsuka Station|Yatsuka Station]] on the [[:en:Tobu Isesaki Line|Tōbu Isesaki Line]] ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtori taisha|Ōtori taisha]] == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11442008}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]]

{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Hanahata|WD=Q11615595}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.79930278|N|139.81150556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Adachi, Tokyo|Adachi]], Tokyo. {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Hanahata|WD=Q11615595}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.79930278|N|139.81150556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Adachi, Tokyo|en|Adachi, Tokyo|WD=Q213464|lt=Adachi}}, Tokyo. It serves as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami}} (guardian deity) of the Hanahata area and is also known as '''Hanahata Ōtori Shrine''' (花畑大鷲神社). It is considered the origin of the well-known {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} markets, with its head shrine being {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori taisha}}. The shrine is featured in the {{ill|Edo meisho zue|en|Edo meisho zue|WD=Q2704637|lt=Edo meisho zue}}.{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=521}} == Overview == Surrounded by old and large trees, the shrine is situated in a serene environment teeming with various seasonal flora and fauna. Though typically tranquil, it attracts many visitors during {{ill|Hatsumōde|en|Hatsumōde|WD=Q1032381|lt=Hatsumōde}} (New Year’s visits) and {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San}} celebrations in November. During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ({{ill|Sangaku|en|Sangaku|WD=Q2569069|lt=Sangaku}}) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the {{ill|Hōji (era)|en|Hōji (era)|WD=Q1327400|lt=Hōji}} period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the {{ill|Gosannen War|en|Gosannen War|WD=Q1187411|lt=Later Three-Year War}}, the Heian-period military commander {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|WD=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}} prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan’ei}} (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|WD=Q1145295|lt=Manji}} (1659). * During the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|1=Shogakuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545644}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the {{ill|Kubota Domain|en|Kubota Domain|WD=Q1067720|lt=Kubota Domain}}’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|en|Hidari Jingorō|WD=Q2668220|lt=Hidari Jingorō}}. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|WD=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}} was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ({{ill|Marine Day|en|Marine Day|WD=Q1355833|lt=Marine Day}}): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from {{ill|Yatsuka Station|en|Yatsuka Station|WD=Q8050280|lt=Yatsuka Station}} on the {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|en|Tobu Isesaki Line|WD=Q906598|lt=Tōbu Isesaki Line}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title={{ill|Edo meisho zue|en|Edo meisho zue|WD=Q2704637|lt=Edo Meisho Zue}}|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori taisha}} == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11442008}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]]

Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|en|Hidari Jingorō|WD=Q2668220|lt=Hidari Jingorō}}. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|WD=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}} was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ({{ill|Marine Day|en|Marine Day|WD=Q1355833|lt=Marine Day}}): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from {{ill|Yatsuka Station|en|Yatsuka Station|WD=Q8050280|lt=Yatsuka Station}} on the {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|en|Tobu Isesaki Line|WD=Q906598|lt=Tōbu Isesaki Line}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title={{ill|Edo meisho zue|en|Edo meisho zue|WD=Q2704637|lt=Edo Meisho Zue}}|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori taisha}} == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11442008}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Removing {{afc comment}} templates
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)]][[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[co:commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)]][[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)]][[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōtori Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ootori jinja hanahata adachi 2014.jpg | location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Adachi, Tokyo|en|Adachi, Tokyo|WD=Q213464|lt=Adachi}}, {{ill|Hanahata (Adachi)|ja|花畑 (足立区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata (Adachi)|1=Hanahata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615595}} 7-15-1 | Latitude = 35 | Latitude_min = 48 | Latitude_sec = 33.5 | Longitude = 139 | Longitude_min = 49 | Longitude_sec = 13 | Label_position = bottom | Map = Tokyo city | deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} | Establishment = {{ill|Hōji (era)|en|Hōji (era)|WD=Q1327400|lt=Hōji}} period (1247–1249) | Main_building = | Alternate_names = Hanahata Ōtori Shrine | Festival = | Ceremonies = {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}}
{{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Hanahata|WD=Q11615595}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.79930278|N|139.81150556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ōtori Shrine''' (大鷲神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Adachi, Tokyo|en|Adachi, Tokyo|WD=Q213464|lt=Adachi}}, Tokyo. It serves as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami}} (guardian deity) of the Hanahata area and is also known as '''Hanahata Ōtori Shrine''' (花畑大鷲神社). It is considered the origin of the well-known {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} markets, with its head shrine being {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori taisha}}. The shrine is featured in the {{ill|Edo meisho zue|en|Edo meisho zue|WD=Q2704637|lt=Edo meisho zue}}.{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=521}} == Overview == Surrounded by old and large trees, the shrine is situated in a serene environment teeming with various seasonal flora and fauna. Though typically tranquil, it attracts many visitors during {{ill|Hatsumōde|en|Hatsumōde|WD=Q1032381|lt=Hatsumōde}} (New Year’s visits) and {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|en|Shichi-Go-San|WD=Q1072387|lt=Shichi-Go-San}} celebrations in November. During the nights of {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}}, fireworks are displayed. The shrine also preserves records of mathematical tablets ({{ill|Sangaku|en|Sangaku|WD=Q2569069|lt=Sangaku}}) dedicated by local Edo-period mathematicians. Among these, the "Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku," donated by Kanesugi Kiyotoki’s disciples in 1833, is registered as a tangible cultural property of Adachi Ward.{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.adachi.tokyo.jp/bunka/chiikibunka/bunkazai_ootorijinjasangaku.html|title=Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Sangaku|publisher=Adachi Ward|accessdate=2022-08-22}} == History == === Origins === The shrine's founding is attributed to the {{ill|Hōji (era)|en|Hōji (era)|WD=Q1327400|lt=Hōji}} period (1247–1249).{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=231}} It is said that during the {{ill|Gosannen War|en|Gosannen War|WD=Q1187411|lt=Later Three-Year War}}, the Heian-period military commander {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|WD=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}} prayed for victory at this location after witnessing an eagle. This marked the shrine’s establishment.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1992|p=162}} Initially referred to as {{ill|Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja|鷲明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was renamed Ōtori Shrine following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} during the Meiji era. * The shrine is mentioned in a {{ill|Munafuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōda|qq=}} dated to the first year of {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan’ei}} (1624), indicating its existence prior to this date. * The oldest extant stone artifacts date back to the second year of {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|WD=Q1145295|lt=Manji}} (1659). * During the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} period, the shrine’s affiliated temple was {{ill|Shōkaku-in|ja|正覚院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkaku-in|1=Shogakuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545644}}. * The current shrine structure dates back to the Ansei era, with contributions from the {{ill|Kubota Domain|en|Kubota Domain|WD=Q1067720|lt=Kubota Domain}}’s Satake family. Its wooden columns feature carvings attributed to a disciple of {{ill|Hidari Jingorō|en|Hidari Jingorō|WD=Q2668220|lt=Hidari Jingorō}}. === deity === While {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} is listed as the shrine’s current deity, historical records suggest that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|WD=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}} was likely its original object of worship. The shrine received significant patronage from the Satake family and other clans claiming descent from Yoshimitsu. == Events == * '''Third Sunday of July''' ({{ill|Marine Day|en|Marine Day|WD=Q1355833|lt=Marine Day}}): {{ill|Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja|花畑大鷲神社獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanahata Ōtori Shrine Lion Dance|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated an intangible cultural property of Adachi Ward). The shrine’s {{ill|Three-Lion Dance|ja|三匹獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-Lion Dance|qq=}} is performed.{{sfn|Adachi Historical Society|1978|p=232}} * {{ill|Tori-no-Ichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} == Access == * A 27-minute walk from {{ill|Yatsuka Station|en|Yatsuka Station|WD=Q8050280|lt=Yatsuka Station}} on the {{ill|Tobu Isesaki Line|en|Tobu Isesaki Line|WD=Q906598|lt=Tōbu Isesaki Line}} ({{OSM route map|start=35.81411,139.80140|goal=35.80924,139.82021|name=Route}}). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Adachi Historical Society. *History of Adachi Ward*. Meicho Publishing, 1978, pp. 230–232. * Adachi Historical Society (ed.). *Adachi Ward Historical Walks*. Gakuseisha, 1992, pp. 162–166. * {{cite book|title={{ill|Edo meisho zue|en|Edo meisho zue|WD=Q2704637|lt=Edo Meisho Zue}}|volume=3|author=Yukio Saitō|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|year=1927|chapter=巻之六 開陽之部 正一位大鷲大明神社|pages=521–524|id={{NDLJP|1174157/265}}|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōtori taisha|en|Ōtori taisha|WD=Q705151|lt=Ōtori taisha}} == External Links == * [http://ootori-jinja.or.jp/ Official Ōtori Shrine Website] {{Ōtori Faith}} {{translated page|ja|大鷲神社 (足立区)|version=103732014|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大鷲神社 (足立区)]] [[ja:大鷲神社]] {{Commons category|Ōtori-jinja (Hanahata, Adachi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11442008}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshimitsu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Adachi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]]
